title: Cinnamon Sweet Potato & Maple Pecan Bowl
subtitle: Sweet, earthy & caramelised
image_prompt: A cosy autumn grain bowl of cinnamon-roasted sweet potato cubes and fluffy quinoa, topped with glossy maple-candied pecans, ruby pomegranate seeds, crumbled feta and fresh mint, drizzled with maple-tahini, natural light, top-down food photography

steps.title: Roast the sweet potato
steps.text: Peel and cube the sweet potato.

steps.title: Toss with cinnamon
steps.text: Toss with olive oil, cinnamon and salt, then roast at 200°C for 25–30 minutes until caramelised at the edges.
steps.tip: Cut even cubes so they roast at the same rate.

steps.title: Cook the quinoa
steps.text: Rinse the quinoa and cook in double the amount of water for 15 minutes, then fluff with a fork.

steps.title: Candy the pecans
steps.text: Toast pecans in a dry pan for 2 minutes, add 1 tbsp maple syrup and a pinch of cinnamon, toss for 1 minute until glossy, then tip onto baking paper to cool.
steps.tip: They turn from sticky to crisp as they cool — don't skip the cooling.

steps.title: Make maple-tahini sauce
steps.text: Whisk tahini and maple syrup with a splash of water until smooth and pourable.

steps.title: Build the bowl
steps.text: Layer the quinoa and roasted sweet potato in bowls.

steps.title: Top & drizzle
steps.text: Top with pomegranate, crumbled feta (if using), candied pecans and mint, then drizzle with maple-tahini.
steps.kidsHelp: Kids can scatter the pecans and pomegranate! 🧒

title: Cinnamon Roll Pecan Yogurt Bowl
subtitle: Like a cinnamon roll, made wholesome
image_prompt: A creamy Greek yogurt breakfast bowl swirled with cinnamon, topped with sliced banana, toasted chopped pecans and a glossy date-caramel drizzle and almond butter, bright natural light, top-down food photography

steps.title: Toast the pecans
steps.text: Toast the pecans in a dry pan for 2–3 minutes until fragrant, then roughly chop.
steps.tip: Watch closely — nuts go from golden to burnt fast.

steps.title: Make the date caramel
steps.text: Pit the dates, soak in 3 tbsp hot water for 5 minutes, then blend with a pinch of cinnamon and salt until smooth, loosening with water as needed.
steps.tip: No blender? Mash soft dates with a fork into a paste.

steps.title: Spoon the yogurt
steps.text: Spoon the Greek yogurt into bowls and swirl through a little cinnamon.

steps.title: Add the banana
steps.text: Slice the banana over the yogurt.

steps.title: Drizzle & finish
steps.text: Drizzle with date caramel and almond butter, top with toasted pecans and a little maple syrup.
steps.kidsHelp: Kids can drizzle the caramel and scatter the pecans! 🧒

title: Apple Pie Pecan Oat Bowl
subtitle: Warm, cosy & tastes like dessert
image_prompt: A warm creamy oat porridge bowl topped with golden cinnamon-sautéed apple cubes, toasted pecans and raisins, drizzled with maple syrup and almond butter, steam rising, cosy natural light, top-down food photography

steps.title: Cook the porridge
steps.text: Simmer the oats with oat milk, a pinch of salt and ½ tsp cinnamon for 5–7 minutes, stirring, until creamy.
steps.tip: A splash more oat milk at the end loosens it to your liking.

steps.title: Sauté the cinnamon apples
steps.text: Cube the apples and cook in a little butter or coconut oil with cinnamon and 1 tbsp maple syrup for 4–5 minutes until soft and golden.
steps.kidsHelp: Kids can stir the cinnamon apples! 🧒

steps.title: Toast the pecans
steps.text: Toast the pecans in a dry pan for 2–3 minutes, then roughly chop.

steps.title: Build & serve
steps.text: Spoon the porridge into bowls, top with the cinnamon apples, pecans and raisins, then drizzle with maple syrup and almond butter.
